expectations.text: Identifies passive voice overuse: 'was implemented', 'were run', 'was completed', 'was discovered', 'was applied' and suggests active voice rewrites
expectations.evidence: Review report lines 41 and 28 identify all five passive constructions ('was implemented,' 'were run,' 'was completed,' 'was discovered,' 'was applied') and line 62 suggests active voice rewrites: 'The team implemented OAuth2 login. CI runs tests on every push. We deployed to staging on Tuesday.'

expectations.text: Identifies generic analogies: 'Swiss Army knife for developers', 'GPS for your codebase', 'bridge between frontend and backend'
expectations.evidence: Review report line 42 explicitly lists all three: '"Like a Swiss Army knife for developers," "Think of it as a GPS for your codebase," "Consider it a bridge between frontend and backend"' categorized as 'Generic analogies'.

expectations.text: Identifies meta-commentary: 'In this tutorial, we will discuss', 'Let's explore'
expectations.evidence: Review report lines 39-40 identify both: '"In this tutorial, we will discuss" -- meta-commentary opening' and '"Let’s explore the various options available" -- more meta-commentary with no substance'.

expectations.text: Identifies shallow insights: 'significantly improves developer productivity by streamlining common workflows' contains no specific facts
expectations.evidence: Review report line 43 flags lines 13-15 as 'Vague corporate language with no specifics. What tool? What workflows? How much improvement?' categorized as 'Perfect grammar, shallow insight'. Tier 2 also flags 'streamlining' as an AI buzzword. Recommended fix #4 says 'Replace buzzwords with specifics: name the tool, state actual performance numbers, describe the real workflow improvement.'

expectations.text: Identifies false enthusiasm: 'thrilled to announce', 'exciting new feature', 'How amazing is that?' with excessive exclamation marks
expectations.evidence: Review report line 44 identifies '"We’re thrilled to announce," "exciting new feature," "You’re going to love," "How amazing is that?" -- excessive enthusiasm with no substance' categorized as 'Artificial enthusiasm / hype'. Recommended fix #5 says 'Remove hype... without exclamation marks or enthusiasm markers.'

expectations.text: Identifies excessive hedging: 'may potentially', 'some users', 'certain situations', 'somewhat better', 'in many cases'
expectations.evidence: Review report line 45 lists all hedging phrases: '"may potentially," "some users," "certain situations," "somewhat better," "many cases," "depending on"' categorized as 'Excessive hedging'. Tier 3 analysis also calls out the hedging paragraph separately.

suggestions: The review could quote the exact source text more completely when flagging shallow insights (e.g., include the full sentence 'significantly improves developer productivity by streamlining common workflows').
Consider adding severity ratings to each finding to help prioritize fixes.
eval_feedback.overall: Excellent performance. The skill identified all six categories of AI writing anti-patterns with specific line references, quoted examples, and actionable fix recommendations. The multi-tier analysis framework provided thorough coverage from character-level through voice-level review.
